structural_claim: Spontaneous symmetry breaking generates mass and distinguishes phases of matter through the emergence of order parameters with non-zero vacuum expectation values

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Phase transitions occur through spontaneous symmetry breaking characterized by order parameters
Superconductivity emerges from spontaneous gauge symmetry breaking with a complex order parameter
Fundamental particle masses arise from spontaneous electroweak symmetry breaking via a scalar field with non-zero vacuum expectation value
The distinction between electromagnetic and weak forces emerges from spontaneous breaking of SU(2)×U(1) symmetry
